name: RichTextEditor
description: A feature-rich WYSIWYG text editor built on TipTap with StarterKit extensions. Provides formatting tools including bold, italic, strikethrough, code, headings, lists, blockquotes, code blocks, and horizontal rules. Includes built-in toolbar with undo/redo functionality and supports custom toolbar configuration.
usage_guidelines: Use for content creation, blog posts, comments, documentation, or any rich text input needs.
Provide initial content via content prop (HTML string).
Handle content changes via onChange callback which provides text, HTML, and JSON formats.
Customize toolbar with customToolBar render prop for specific use cases.
Use customEditor prop to provide your own TipTap editor instance with custom extensions.
Set invalid prop to show error state styling.
Style editor content area with editorContentClass prop.
Access editor ref for programmatic control.

content.description: Initial editor content as HTML string. Updates will sync with editor.

onChange.type: (content: { text: string, html: string, json: JSONContent }) => void
onChange.description: Callback fired when content changes. Receives content in three formats: plain text, HTML, and TipTap JSON.

customToolBar.description: Custom toolbar renderer. Receives editor instance and all available tool button components. Return custom toolbar layout.

customEditor.description: Custom TipTap editor instance. Use this to provide editor with custom extensions or configuration.

invalid.description: Shows error state styling with red border and background.

editorContentClass.description: Additional CSS classes for editor content area.

ref.description: Ref to editor container div element.

default_toolbar.description: Default toolbar includes these formatting tools
default_toolbar.note: Undo/Redo buttons available but not included in default toolbar

StarterKit.description: Default TipTap extension bundle including most basic formatting

configuration.bulletList: { keepMarks: true } - Preserves marks when creating lists
configuration.orderedList: { keepMarks: true } - Preserves marks when creating lists

json.note: Useful for storing in database and restoring exact editor state

styling_info.method: Tailwind CSS with TipTap prose classes
styling_info.visual_description: Rounded container with border. Toolbar at top with formatting buttons. Main editor area below with prose styling for content. Focus state shows primary color ring. Invalid state shows red border and background. Max height 600px with scroll.
styling_info.container_classes: rounded-lg ring-1 ring-transparent border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-600

Do: Use for content creation interfaces (blogs, comments, documentation)
Provide initial content for editing existing content
Use customToolBar for specific use cases (e.g., minimal toolbar for comments)
Sanitize HTML output before rendering in other contexts
Use JSON format for database storage (preserves exact structure)
Provide clear placeholder or instructions for empty editor
Test with keyboard shortcuts for accessibility
Use customEditor for advanced features (mentions, images, etc.)
Dont: Don't use for simple text input (use Input or Textarea components)
Don't forget to sanitize HTML output to prevent XSS
Don't overload toolbar with too many options
Don't forget to handle paste events (TipTap handles this)
Don't mutate editor content directly (use editor.commands)
Don't forget to handle empty content validation
Don't use without onChange if you need to track content
Don't forget to provide loading state while editor initializes

examples.title: Basic Usage
With Custom Toolbar
With Form Validation
With Custom Editor and Extensions
Saving and Loading Content

custom_extensions.description: Add custom TipTap extensions for advanced features

methods: Use editorContentClass prop for content area styling
Add custom CSS for editor container

integration_notes: Supports all TipTap extensions and configurations
Content syncs automatically when content prop changes
Prose classes provide default typography styling
Keyboard shortcuts work out of the box
Paste handling includes smart formatting cleanup

recommendations: Provide clear labels for toolbar buttons
Include keyboard shortcut hints in tooltips

performance_notes: Content updates are debounced internally by TipTap
Large documents may impact performance (consider pagination)
JSON format is more efficient for storage than HTML
Use React.memo for custom toolbar components
Avoid unnecessary re-renders by memoizing onChange handler
